---
title: "Chapter 01. A Minimal Quant Research Loop"
subtitle: "Overview"
---

# Objective

This chapter introduces the minimum loop behind a testable trading idea:

1. define a signal
2. convert the signal into tradable positions
3. simulate net returns
4. review the result against risk and cost assumptions

# Sections

## 1.1 Research Loop

[Open section](sections/01-research-loop.qmd)

The first section explains the shape of the research loop and the decision
points where false confidence usually enters a backtest.

## 1.2 Net Returns and Costs

[Open section](sections/02-net-returns-costs.qmd)

The second section separates gross signal behavior from transaction costs,
slippage, and position timing.

# Output Links

The same topic also has presentation-oriented outputs:

- [Video slides](../../slides/video-lesson.qmd)
- [Web notes](../../handouts/web-notes.qmd)
- [PDF notes](../../handouts/pdf-notes.qmd)
